# UC-18.1.11 · Cisco ACI Fault Lifecycle, MTTR, Stuck-Clear, and Storm Detection

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Observability &middot; **Type:** Fault, Operational, Availability &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We stitch each trouble item's stages from first flicker through clear-out, so crews spot tickets frozen halfway or never finishing—not just how many warnings flash at once.*

---

## Description

Tracks individual **Cisco ACI** **faultInst** / **faultRecord** managed objects through **lc** lifecycles (**soaking**, **raised**, **raised-clearing**, **retaining**, **retaining-acknowl**, **cleared**) to compute **MTTR**-style durations from **`created`** through clearing transitions, flag **raised** faults stale beyond **24h**, detect coordinated **fault storms** (**>50** raises inside **5** minutes per **APIC** node), and highlight recurring **code**+**dn** pairs with **`occur`** spikes—complementing UC-18.1.2 severity volume analytics without duplicating raw count dashboards.

## Value

Leadership reporting gains trustworthy **repair-interval** narratives tied to real controller state machines instead of cherry-picked counters—accelerating escalations when faults linger in **raised**, justifying capacity work when bursts cluster by **APIC**, and giving problem management evidence when the same **MO** path rejects fixes repeatedly as **occur** climbs.

## Implementation

Land **Splunk_TA_cisco-aci** syslog plus **rest_ta** pulls for **`faultInst`**, **`faultRecord`**, optional **`faultDelegate`**, and **aaaModLR** audits into **`cisco:aci:fault`**, **`cisco:aci:fault_record`**, **`cisco:aci:syslog`**, **`cisco:aci:audit`**, and **`cisco:aci:health`** indexes; normalize **FIELDALIAS** for **lc** and **lastTransition**; schedule lifecycle, MTTR, stuck-clear, and storm saved searches with change-aware throttles.

## Detailed Implementation

### Prerequisites
- APIC 6.0(x) / 6.1(x) clusters expose **`/api/class/faultInst.json`**, **`/api/class/faultRecord.json`**, and **`/api/class/faultDelegate.json`** over HTTPS 443/tcp—provision read-only REST operators with RBAC scoped to read fv and infra MIT branches used by your collectors, not full admin personas.
- Install Splunk Add-on for Cisco ACI packaged as **Splunk_TA_cisco-aci** (**Splunkbase** 4022) where APIC and Nexus syslog terminate so **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:syslog`** carries mnemonic bursts that align with **`faultInst`** code families (F0103, F0467, F0613, F1394) without replacing lc truth from REST.
- Add Splunk_REST_API_Modular_Input (**rest_ta**, **Splunkbase** 1546) GET templates for **`/api/class/faultInst.json`** (live MO rows), **`/api/class/faultRecord.json`** (historical clears), optional **`/api/class/faultDelegate.json`** (delegation chains), and **`/api/class/aaaModLR.json`** or audit feeds mirroring POST acknowledgements—target **`index=cisco_aci`** (rename per governance) and sourcetypes **`cisco:aci:fault`**, **`cisco:aci:fault_record`**, **`cisco:aci:audit`**, aligning **FIELDALIAS** maps for camelCase drift on **`created`**, **`lastTransition`**, `severity`, `lc`, `occur`, `changeSet`, `rule`, and `delegated`.
- Poll **faultInst** every 120–300s on busy fabrics to balance APIC CPU with lifecycle fidelity; faster cadence improves soaking detection, slower cadence risks smoothing **raised-clearing** flaps that syslog still shouts about.
- Map **fvFaultLifecycleP** defaults (roughly five-day retention for cleared records) versus per-code **`fvFaultLcP`** soak overrides so retaining versus cleared timestamps you compute as MTT endpoints match the controller—not an assumed clock.
- Confirm Splunk indexers, heavy forwarders, and credential vaults honor TLS without inspection boxes that rewrite JSON payloads mid-stream.

### Step 1 — Configure data collection for lifecycle fidelity (not volume alone)
- Live fault lane: **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:fault`** via **`GET /api/class/faultInst.json`** with optional **`query-target-filter`** predicates to trim noisy tenants during labs—each poll should preserve **`dn`**, **`code`**, `severity`, `lc`, `cause`, **`created`**, **`lastTransition`**, `occur`, `changeSet`, `rule`, and `delegated` attributes exactly as APIC returns them.
- History lane: **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:fault_record`** via **`GET /api/class/faultRecord.json`** using time-bounded **`created`** filters after UTC normalization—use this lane to prove cleared outcomes that disappeared from **faultInst** yet remain operationally argumentative in reviews.
- Syslog lane: **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:syslog`** from TA parsing—pair with REST because PRI shifts alone cannot reconstruct **soaking → raised → raised-clearing → retaining → retaining-acknowl** ordering.
- Audit lane: **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:audit`** (**aaaModLR**) capturing acknowledgement POST bodies with **`ackUser`**, **`ackCause`**, **`ackTime`** equivalents—required if you report human time-to-ack alongside **MTTR** from lifecycle transitions.
- Health lane (correlation): **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:health`** from fabric score snapshots—useful when **MTTR** elongations coincide with spine saturation even if fault codes differ across MO classes.

### Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Paste after confirming five sourcetypes land in **`index=cisco_aci`** with fields populated—extend **`coalesce()`** chains if collectors renamed keys during TA upgrades.

```spl
index=cisco_aci (sourcetype="cisco:aci:fault" OR sourcetype="cisco:aci:fault_record") earliest=-7d@m latest=now
| eval lc_state=lower(trim(coalesce(lc,fault_lc,"")))
| eval code_u=upper(trim(coalesce(code,fault_code,faultCode,"")))
| eval dn_k=lower(trim(coalesce(dn,distinguished_name,"")))
| eval apic_id=lower(trim(coalesce(apic,apic_name,host,"fabric")))
| eval sev=lower(trim(coalesce(severity,fault_severity,faultSeverity,"")))
| eval ts_raise=coalesce(strptime(created,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3N"),strptime(created,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S"))
| eval ts_last=coalesce(strptime(lastTransition,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3N"),strptime(lastTransition,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S"))
| eval occur_n=tonumber(coalesce(occur,"1"))
| eval delegated_f=lower(trim(coalesce(delegated,"no")))
| eval is_delegate=if(delegated_f="yes" OR delegated_f="true",1,0)
| eval mo_hint=lower(trim(coalesce(classId,mo_class,object_type,"faultInst")))
| eval rule_tag=coalesce(rule,"")
| eval change_snip=substr(coalesce(changeSet,""),1,96)
| where isnotnull(code_u) AND code_u!="" AND isnotnull(dn_k) AND dn_k!=""
| eval in_clearing_lane=if(lc_state IN ("raised-clearing","retaining","retaining-acknowl"),1,0)
| eval is_cleared=if(lc_state="cleared" OR sev="cleared",1,0)
| eval mttr_sec=if((in_clearing_lane=1 OR is_cleared=1) AND isnotnull(ts_raise) AND isnotnull(ts_last), ts_last-ts_raise, null())
| eval hours_since_last=if(isnotnull(ts_last), round((now()-ts_last)/3600,2), null())
| eval stuck_in_raised=if(lc_state="raised" AND hours_since_last>24 AND match(sev,"(critical|major)"),1,0)
| bin span=5m _time
| eventstats count as raises_in_5m by apic_id _time
| stats latest(lc_state) as lifecycle latest(sev) as severity latest(mo_hint) as mo_class latest(ts_raise) as created_ts latest(ts_last) as last_transition_ts latest(rule_tag) as rule_name latest(change_snip) as change_excerpt max(mttr_sec) as mttr_seconds max(hours_since_last) as hours_since_trans max(stuck_in_raised) as stuck_open max(raises_in_5m) as burst_peak max(is_delegate) as delegation_seen max(occur_n) as occur_max latest(cause) as cause_latest by dn_k code_u
| eval mttr_min=if(isnotnull(mttr_seconds), round(mttr_seconds/60,2), null())
| eval fault_storm=if(burst_peak>50,1,0)
| eval recurring=if(occur_max>5,1,0)
| eval never_cleared_story=if(lifecycle="raised" AND stuck_open=1,1,0)
| where stuck_open=1 OR fault_storm=1 OR recurring=1 OR never_cleared_story=1 OR (isnotnull(mttr_min) AND mttr_min>=15 AND match(severity,"(critical|major)"))
| sort 0 - stuck_open - fault_storm - mttr_seconds
| head 300
| table dn_k code_u lifecycle mo_class severity mttr_min hours_since_trans stuck_open fault_storm recurring occur_max delegation_seen rule_name change_excerpt cause_latest created_ts last_transition_ts
```

#### Understanding this SPL
- State focus: unlike **UC-18.1.2** volume dashboards, this pipeline keeps **`lifecycle`** (lc) per `dn_k` + `code_u` and derives repair-interval style durations (**`mttr_min`**) when the fault enters **`raised-clearing`**, `retaining`, or **`retaining-acknowl`** lanes (including `cleared` severity rows from **`cisco:aci:fault_record`**).
- Stuck logic flags `raised` critical/major rows whose **`lastTransition`** is older than 24h, signaling remediation drift or a stalled collector.
- Fault storm logic bins `_time` into 5-minute buckets per `apic_id`; more than 50 events in a window points to coordinated raises deserving a bridge—not a lone line card.
- Recurring faults use **`occur_max > 5`** to spotlight chronic **`code`**, **`dn`** pairs even when severity looks modest individually.
- **`delegation_seen`** warns when `delegated` attributes mark faults bubbled from child MO paths—exclude double counts in downstream KPI math.
- Tune **`fault_storm`** thresholds per pod density; **`mttr_min>=15`** filter trims noise for major/critical only—relax for labs.

Pipeline walkthrough (selected stages)
- Scopes **`index=cisco_aci`** across **`cisco:aci:fault`** and **`cisco:aci:fault_record`** so both active **faultInst** and historical clears influence **MTTR** math.
- Normalizes **`lc_state`** to track soaking, raised, **raised-clearing**, retaining, **retaining-acknowl**, and cleared outcomes consistently across transports.
- Derives **`mttr_sec`** by subtracting parsed **`created`** from **`lastTransition`** whenever the row sits in clearing or retained families—this approximates operator-facing mean time to repair for automation-friendly cohorts.
- Uses **`eventstats`** to compute **`raises_in_5m`** per APIC identity, implementing the >50 faults in five minutes storm heuristic on a per-node basis.
- `stats` collapses repeated polls to the latest **`lifecycle`** snapshot per `dn_k`, `code_u`, while preserving max `occur`, delegation hints, rule, and `changeSet` snippets for runbook starters.

### Step 3 — Validate against APIC UI truth
- Open APIC → System → Faults and locate the same **`dn`**—compare Lifecycle badge strings against Splunk **`lifecycle`**; mismatch usually means duplicated transports or stale modular input tokens.
- Cross-check **`lastTransition`** in JSON from **`moquery`** against Splunk `last_transition_ts`; widespread skew hints NTP drift or frozen REST sessions stuck on **`aaaRefresh`** failures.
- Pick one critical fault stuck in `raised`: confirm whether `occur` increments in APIC while Splunk shows static rows—if so, widen poll frequency or inspect 429 throttling in **`splunkd.log`** for **rest_ta**.
- Compare **`cisco:aci:fault_record`** counts near retention boundaries with **`fvFaultLifecycleP`**—Splunk may still hold JSON after APIC pruned **faultRecord** objects from interactive panes.

### Step 4 — Operationalize dashboards and runbooks
- Dashboard row A: timeline of **`lifecycle`** transitions per `dn_k` with `timechart` overlays of **`cisco:aci:health`** scores to narrate customer impact beside fault codes.
- Dashboard row B: table sorted by **`stuck_open`** then **`fault_storm`** with columns **`mttr_min`**, **`occur_max`**, **`delegation_seen`**, `rule_name`, `change_excerpt` for bridge readability.
- Dashboard row C: single-value **`median(mttr_min)`** for critical cohorts plus count of **`never_cleared_story`** tickets awaiting owner assignment.
- Publish saved searches for paging: (1) **`stuck_open=1`** for SEV-1 rotation, (2) **`fault_storm=1`** after maintenance correlation, (3) `recurring=1` for problem management reviews—throttle using change lookup windows to avoid duplicate bridges.

### Step 5 — Visualization, alert design, and troubleshooting
- Visualization: pair duration histograms of **`mttr_min`** with scatter plots of `hours_since_trans` versus **`occur_max`** to separate flaky optics from one-off misconfigurations; add sankey-style exports offline (Splunk natively favors timeline) by exporting **`transaction`** results for executive summaries.
- Alert design: include `dn_k`, `code_u`, **`lifecycle`**, `severity`, `cause_latest`, and direct MIT hyperlinks in the payload; route delegation heavy storms to tenant owners using extracted `uni/tn-` segments via `rex` in downstream macros.
- Troubleshooting: No fresh `_time`—verify **rest_ta** endpoint, password rotation, and TLS trust stores; **`lifecycle` blank**—check **FIELDALIAS** after TA upgrades; **`mttr_min` always null**—confirm **`created`** parses (some exports omit fractional seconds); **`fault_storm` never fires**—validate five-minute bin alignment versus timezone overrides; duplicate rows—dedupe `dn_k`, `code_u`, `last_transition_ts` fingerprints when webhook plus REST replay the same snapshot; health lane empty—reenable **`cisco:aci:health`** poller tied to **`HDfabricOverallHealth5min-0`** style endpoints if your TA version supports it.

Anchors — **faultInst**, **faultRecord**, **faultDelegate**, lc, severity, created, **lastTransition**, occur, **retaining-acknowl**, **raised-clearing**, **MTTR**, MTTA, Splunk_TA_cisco-aci, **rest_ta**.

## SPL

```spl
index=cisco_aci (sourcetype="cisco:aci:fault" OR sourcetype="cisco:aci:fault_record") earliest=-7d@m latest=now
| eval lc_state=lower(trim(coalesce(lc,fault_lc,"")))
| eval code_u=upper(trim(coalesce(code,fault_code,faultCode,"")))
| eval dn_k=lower(trim(coalesce(dn,distinguished_name,"")))
| eval apic_id=lower(trim(coalesce(apic,apic_name,host,"fabric")))
| eval sev=lower(trim(coalesce(severity,fault_severity,faultSeverity,"")))
| eval ts_raise=coalesce(strptime(created,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3N"),strptime(created,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S"))
| eval ts_last=coalesce(strptime(lastTransition,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3N"),strptime(lastTransition,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S"))
| eval occur_n=tonumber(coalesce(occur,"1"))
| eval delegated_f=lower(trim(coalesce(delegated,"no")))
| eval is_delegate=if(delegated_f="yes" OR delegated_f="true",1,0)
| eval mo_hint=lower(trim(coalesce(classId,mo_class,object_type,"faultInst")))
| eval rule_tag=coalesce(rule,"")
| eval change_snip=substr(coalesce(changeSet,""),1,96)
| where isnotnull(code_u) AND code_u!="" AND isnotnull(dn_k) AND dn_k!=""
| eval in_clearing_lane=if(lc_state IN ("raised-clearing","retaining","retaining-acknowl"),1,0)
| eval is_cleared=if(lc_state="cleared" OR sev="cleared",1,0)
| eval mttr_sec=if((in_clearing_lane=1 OR is_cleared=1) AND isnotnull(ts_raise) AND isnotnull(ts_last), ts_last-ts_raise, null())
| eval hours_since_last=if(isnotnull(ts_last), round((now()-ts_last)/3600,2), null())
| eval stuck_in_raised=if(lc_state="raised" AND hours_since_last>24 AND match(sev,"(critical|major)"),1,0)
| bin span=5m _time
| eventstats count as raises_in_5m by apic_id _time
| stats latest(lc_state) as lifecycle latest(sev) as severity latest(mo_hint) as mo_class latest(ts_raise) as created_ts latest(ts_last) as last_transition_ts latest(rule_tag) as rule_name latest(change_snip) as change_excerpt max(mttr_sec) as mttr_seconds max(hours_since_last) as hours_since_trans max(stuck_in_raised) as stuck_open max(raises_in_5m) as burst_peak max(is_delegate) as delegation_seen max(occur_n) as occur_max latest(cause) as cause_latest by dn_k code_u
| eval mttr_min=if(isnotnull(mttr_seconds), round(mttr_seconds/60,2), null())
| eval fault_storm=if(burst_peak>50,1,0)
| eval recurring=if(occur_max>5,1,0)
| eval never_cleared_story=if(lifecycle="raised" AND stuck_open=1,1,0)
| where stuck_open=1 OR fault_storm=1 OR recurring=1 OR never_cleared_story=1 OR (isnotnull(mttr_min) AND mttr_min>=15 AND match(severity,"(critical|major)"))
| sort 0 - stuck_open - fault_storm - mttr_seconds
| head 300
| table dn_k code_u lifecycle mo_class severity mttr_min hours_since_trans stuck_open fault_storm recurring occur_max delegation_seen rule_name change_excerpt cause_latest created_ts last_transition_ts
```

## Visualization

(1) **Timeline** or **timechart** of **lc** transitions per **dn** beside **health** lanes. (2) **Histogram** of **mttr_min** for **critical**/**major** cohorts. (3) Table ranking **stuck_open**, **fault_storm**, **recurring**. (4) **Single-value** medians for **MTTR** with drilldowns to **rule**/**changeSet** excerpts. (5) Optional heatmap of **delegation_seen** bursts by **tenant**.

## Known False Positives

**lc_poll_blind_spot** — Sub-minute soaking churn or rapid raised-clearing oscillations between REST polls can hide transitions that syslog shows, so Splunk may under-count dwell time unless you shorten rest_ta intervals or blend cisco:aci:syslog narratives for the same code and dn. Pair shortened polls with Splunk-side max(_time) freshness checks so silent collectors do not masquerade as healthy fabrics.

**retention_timer_rollover** — faultRecord rows age out per fvFaultLifecycleP while faultInst snapshots still look hot; analysts treat short historical silence as clearance when the MO simply aged out of the history class pull. Cross-check moquery history against Splunk retention before closing executive summaries.

**delegate_double_count** — Summing parent faultDelegate rows with child faultInst volumes double-charges severity during storms; segment dashboards by MO class before interpreting storm heuristics. Teach operators that delegation bubbles describe blast summaries, not independent defects.

**ack_automation_skew** — Service accounts push acknowledgement fields in aaaModLR trails, inflating human MTTA stories unless scripted identities are excluded via lookups. Maintain a service-account suppression table reviewed quarterly.

**fault_storm_maintenance** — APIC maintenance windows and simultaneous line-card reloads legitimately raise dozens of raised faults within minutes; correlate _time with change tickets before paging on storm logic alone. Encode maintenance identifiers into lookup tables that temporarily raise suppression thresholds.

**never_cleared_stale_snapshot** — A stalled modular input replays the same JSON payload; lifecycle stays raised in Splunk even when APIC cleared hours ago until collectors refresh—watch for frozen lastTransition timestamps across sequential polls. Alert when _time gaps exceed twice the configured poll interval without credential errors.

**raised_clearing_flap** — Interfaces bouncing during optics work can park faults in raised-clearing and retaining, producing short mttr_min readings while occur climbs—pair clearance math with occur counters and physical validation. Document optics work orders beside Splunk timelines to defuse false closure narratives.

**change_set_echo** — Automation repeatedly posts benign changeSet deltas that momentarily touch lc without true clearance, creating MTTR noise until you filter scripted subjects through an allowlist. Require human-linked tickets for closure when changeSet subjects match automation patterns.
